io.github.cvc5

Class OmtResult

  • java.lang.Object
    • io.github.cvc5.OmtResult

  • public class OmtResult
extends java.lang.Object
    Encapsulation of a solver OMT result. This is the return value of the API methods: - Solver::optimizeSat() - Solver::optimizeSatNext() which we call optimization queries. This class indicates whether the optimization query returned an optimal solution, a non-optimal (approximate) solution, has no solution, or is unknown.

      • deletePointer

      • equals

        public boolean equals(java.lang.Object r)
        Operator overloading for equality of two OMT results.
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
        Parameters:
        r - The OMT result to compare to for equality.
        Returns:
        True if the OMT results are equal.

      • isNull

        public boolean isNull()
        Returns:
        True if OmtResult is empty, i.e., a nullary OmtResult, and not an actual result returned from an optimization query.

      • isOptimal

        public boolean isOptimal()
        Returns:
        True if the OMT query has an optimal solution.

      • isLimitOptimal

        public boolean isLimitOptimal()
        Returns:
        True if the query has a limit-optimal solution.

      • isNonOptimal

        public boolean isNonOptimal()
        Returns:
        True if the query returned a non-optimal (approximate) result.

      • isUnbounded

        public boolean isUnbounded()
        Returns:
        True if the query is unbounded.

      • isUnsat

        public boolean isUnsat()
        Returns:
        True if the query is unsatisfiable.

      • isUnknown

        public boolean isUnknown()
        Returns:
        True if the outcome is unknown.

      • toString

        protected java.lang.String toString(long pointer)
        Returns:
        A string representation of this result.
